corrections.text: Phys.org returned HTTP 403 to the automated fetcher and was not archived locally. The chief editor verified via WebFetch fallback that all claims attributed to Phys.org — including the 220-230 K central crust temperatures, the up-to-900 K peripheral ring temperatures, the 200 K = 13 years crust age, the 8-10 year resurfacing timescale, the 32 of 400 paterae count, and the 7/20/80 GW progression for P63 — match the article verbatim.

The article reports 32 paterae studied (matching Universe Today and Phys.org); the arXiv abstract uses 'thirty'. The popular write-ups likely draw on counts in the full paper rather than only the abstract; the article does not explicitly flag this small discrepancy.
